title: Behavioral Grounding Framework (BGF): LLM-Grounded Multi-Agent Economic Simulation
description: BGF is an open-source multi-agent economic simulation framework that grounds LLM-based agents in empirical socio-economic microdata from the European Social Survey (ESS Round 11). It provides: (i) a formal framework with closed-form bounds on RLHF cooperative bias (Proposition 1: B_RLHF <= 1 - 1/|A|); (ii) the Behavioral Realism Metric (BRM) and RLHF Bias Index (B_RLHF) as evaluation primitives; (iii) a deterministic rule-based ESS policy (Condition D) that reproduces Eurostat-range Gini (0.325, BCa 95% CI [0.324, 0.325] at N=500, T=30, 10 seeds) and a perfect cross-cultural cooperation gradient (Spearman rho = +1.000 vs ESS-11 trust, replicated against WVS Wave 7 r = +0.977 and Herrmann-Thoeni-Gaechter 2008 PGG contributions rho = +0.886, p = 0.033); (iv) an LLM policy stack with dual SQL/Graph RAG, hierarchical memory, and anti-hallucination output parsing; (v) a Flask + Vue deployment of the simulation as an interactive research tool. The pre-registered 10-seed N=100 confirmatory extension finds the within-Mistral-7B grounded vs ungrounded contrast statistically null on cooperation, Gini, and mean wealth (MWU p > 0.85); the N=500 LLM-scale disambiguator is in progress at time of release. The Phi/P_LLM dissociation is reported as the central empirical finding.
notes: Reproduces with `make reproduce` (CPU-only baselines, ~30 min) or `bash reproduce_paper.sh --figures` (figure-regen only). GPU-dependent LLM experiments require a 16 GB CUDA device (Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 at 4-bit quantization) and are documented in scripts/pipeline_*.sh. Includes 636+ unit and integration tests. Pinned dependencies in requirements.txt (numpy/scipy/torch) and requirements-api.txt (Flask + Vue serving). Container build: see Dockerfile (multi-stage).
